scope: router-ab-ecdsa fixed-role API and constant-time checkpoint

claims: default production API hides codec and driver internals
proof and triple internals require the explicit test-utils oracle feature
runtime participants and thresholds are absent
Client and SigningWorker messages and online inputs cannot cross roles
available, reserved, and committed one-use states cannot be reordered or reused
secret-bearing key shares and presign material expose neither Clone nor Debug

onlinePublicCoin.construction: Client commits SHA-256(domain || uniform 32-byte contribution); SigningWorker samples and persists its uniform 32-byte contribution after admission; finalize opens the Client contribution; XOR is the HKDF entropy input
onlinePublicCoin.excludedClaim: fairness and availability against selective abort

sourceReview.secretLogging: production source guard rejects println, eprintln, dbg, tracing, and log calls
sourceReview.secretTypes: secret-bearing protocol states use Zeroize/ZeroizeOnDrop or explicit Drop zeroization
sourceReview.divisionAndRemainder: purpose-built source occurrences operate only on fixed constants, public indexes, public lengths, or deterministic test inputs

openAssumptions.statement: The heuristic analyzer does not prove whether every compiled conditional branch depends only on public data or an explicit aggregate protocol-validity result.
openAssumptions.promotionImpact: Requires bounded independent review or stronger dataflow tooling before a comprehensive constant-time claim.

openAssumptions.statement: Static native and Wasm opcode inspection does not cover JavaScript engine, Workers runtime, cache, allocator, or microarchitectural timing behavior.
openAssumptions.promotionImpact: No target-runtime constant-time claim is made.
